mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in nonfarm payrolls signals strengthening household income and earnings expectations; when PAYEMS is rising, the mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS 3-month growth has nonpositive association with 20-day S&P 500 forward returns in walk-forward tests, or if positive payroll-trend months do not outperform negative payroll-trend months.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5% and has risen over the prior 3 months, higher discount rates and tighter financial conditions reduce equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5% and its 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns are not below unconditional average returns.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month acceleration in retail sales indicates stronger consumer demand and near-term revenue growth for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth rate exceeds its trailing 12-month growth rate
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS acceleration has a negative walk-forward coefficient for 20-day returns or if accelerated retail-sales periods underperform non-accelerated periods after transaction-cost assumptions.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is accelerating while FEDFUNDS is rising, markets price a persistent tightening response that compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation rate rises and FEDFUNDS 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL acceleration and FEDFUNDS increase state does not predict lower 20-day returns than either condition alone in walk-forward interaction tests.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below their 12-month moving average signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and future construction-linked earnings pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its trailing 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside-crossing episodes are followed by 60-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional mean in walk-forward tests or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A positive monthly surprise proxy from INDPRO momentum signals improving factory utilization and operating leverage, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as cyclical earnings expectations adjust.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO latest monthly growth exceeds its trailing 6-month average monthly growth
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO momentum triggers have nonpositive average 5-day S&P 500 forward returns or if triggered returns are not higher than non-triggered returns over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, labor-market deterioration signals recession risk and earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE deterioration episodes produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or if triggered mean returns exceed unconditional mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: When real output growth is positive while unemployment is falling, the combination signals broad expansion without immediate labor slack deterioration, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly growth is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is negative
failure_condition: Reject if the joint state of positive GDPC1 growth and falling UNRATE fails to outperform all other states over 60 trading days in walk-forward tests or has a negative interaction coefficient.
